skill.name: cluster-management
skill.description: Monitor Proxmox VE cluster nodes, resource usage, quorum status, and infrastructure health.
skill.summary: Monitor Proxmox VE cluster health, node status, resource use, and capacity signals.
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external-command and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found these are prose and Markdown false positives in SKILL.md. No evidence found of executable scripts, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Backticked Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown backticks around Proxmox tool names and workflow steps. These lines document available tools and do not contain shell execution syntax, subprocess calls, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are list items and numbered instructions in Markdown. They contain tool names such as get_nodes and get_node_status, not executable shell or Ruby backtick code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Dismissed as Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive text about nodes, cluster health, quorum, capacity, and maintenance. The file contains no cryptographic algorithm selection, hashing code, encryption code, or security-sensitive credential processing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a 90-line Markdown skill description. The cited lines are natural-language documentation and do not implement or recommend weak cryptographic algorithms.

content.value_statement: Proxmox administrators need a fast way to understand node health, quorum, and capacity. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused cluster checks and operational reports.
actual_capabilities: Lists Proxmox cluster nodes using the documented get_nodes tool.
Checks detailed node status with get_node_status.
Reviews cluster resources across nodes, virtual machines, and containers.
Supports health checks for quorum, utilization, and degraded nodes.
Helps produce cluster health and capacity planning reports.
limitations: Requires connected Proxmox tools that provide cluster and node data.
Does not include remediation scripts or automated infrastructure changes.
Cannot validate live cluster health without current tool responses.
Does not replace vendor documentation for upgrades or maintenance windows.

use_cases.title: Daily Cluster Health Review
use_cases.description: Check all nodes, identify degraded systems, and confirm that the cluster is ready for production workloads.

use_cases.title: Capacity Planning Review
use_cases.description: Compare resource use across nodes and identify where additional capacity or balancing may be needed.

use_cases.title: Maintenance Readiness Check
use_cases.description: Review node status and quorum before planned upgrades, failover tests, or hardware maintenance.

prompt_templates.title: Check Cluster Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the current Proxmox cluster status. List every node and highlight anything offline or degraded.

prompt_templates.title: Review Node Health
prompt_templates.prompt: Check each Proxmox node for CPU, memory, storage, and availability concerns. Summarize the highest priority issues first.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Capacity
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze cluster resource usage across nodes, virtual machines, and containers. Identify bottlenecks and capacity risks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Maintenance Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Proxmox cluster maintenance readiness report with node status, quorum concerns, resource imbalance, and recommended checks before work begins.

output_examples.input: Show me the status of all cluster nodes.
output_examples.output: A node-by-node health summary that identifies online nodes, degraded nodes, utilization concerns, and recommended follow-up checks.
A capacity review that compares CPU, memory, and storage pressure across nodes and calls out systems approaching limits.

output_examples.input: Get a complete cluster infrastructure report.
output_examples.output: A structured operational report covering node health, cluster resources, quorum status, utilization trends, and maintenance recommendations.

best_practices: Run health checks before maintenance, upgrades, and workload migrations.
Review quorum and node availability before changing cluster membership.
Compare resource usage across nodes before making capacity decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a single healthy node means the full cluster is healthy.
Do not plan maintenance without checking quorum and failover readiness.
Do not ignore storage and network pressure when reviewing node capacity.

faq.question: What does this skill help monitor?
faq.answer: It helps review Proxmox VE nodes, resource use, quorum status, and cluster health signals.

faq.question: Does this skill make changes to my cluster?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflows focus on monitoring, analysis, and reporting.

faq.question: Which tools does it describe?
faq.answer: It describes get_nodes, get_node_status, and get_cluster_resources for Proxmox cluster review.

faq.question: Can it diagnose capacity problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide analysis of CPU, memory, storage, and resource distribution when tool data is available.

faq.question: Is it suitable for maintenance planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps review node status, quorum, and resource risks before planned maintenance.

faq.question: What access does it require?
faq.answer: It requires available Proxmox tool responses. The skill file itself does not contain executable code.
